data.content: I'll start `dotnet watch test` which will automatically re-run your tests whenever you save a file change.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to use hot reload to iterate on failing tests. I can see there's a .NET 8.0 MSTest project. The `dotnet watch test` command will re-run tests automatically when files change.

Let me start `dotnet watch test` for them.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-e0e33c1148e84c95a11cc260f37bebf4 && dotnet watch test --project TestProject.csproj 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}

data.content: There's a missing `using System;` directive. I'll fix it — `dotnet watch` will detect the change and re-run automatically.
data.reasoningText: There's a missing `using System;` directive causing a compilation error. Let me fix that first, then hot reload should pick it up.

data.content: The watcher started before the file list was built, so it can't detect changes. Let me restart it.
data.reasoningText: It seems the watcher isn't detecting the file change, possibly because the "Fix the error to continue" mode requires file changes to be detected. Let me check the current state of the file and try to trigger a re-detect. The initial error happened during the watch list generation itself, so the watcher might not have the file list set up to detect changes. Let me stop and restart the watch.
